name: Whole Wheat Tortillas
recipeInstructions:
These tortillas are lower in saturated fat, with no cholesterol and higher in fiber than traditional tortillas prepared with lard and white flour. Cornstarch is used for dusting the tortillas.
1. In a large bowl, stir together the first six ingredients until just combined.
2. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently until smooth (about 3-4 minutes).
3. Divide dough into 12 equal portions. Dust each portion lightly with cornstarch.
4. Working one at a time, roll each portion into a round as thin as possible on a lightly floured surface.
5. Heat a large, very hot griddle or non-stick skillet over high heat until it's nearly smoking.
6. Carefully place the rolled dough rounds onto the heated griddle.
7. Cook for 20-30 seconds on one side, then gently lift and turn with a spatula.
8. Cook the other side for an additional 20-30 seconds, or until brown spots appear.
9. Transfer tortillas to a plate. Repeat with remaining dough portions.
10. Yield: 12 large tortillas
